uses: Boosts vegetative growth; increases leaf and stem development; corrects nitrogen deficiency
description: Urea is the most widely used nitrogenous fertilizer in India, containing 46% nitrogen. It is highly soluble and rapidly absorbed by plants, making it ideal for top-dressing.

uses: Promotes strong root development; enhances early plant establishment; supplies phosphorus and nitrogen
description: Di-Ammonium Phosphate (DAP) is one of the most popular phosphatic fertilizers in India. It provides both nitrogen and phosphorus in a highly available form, making it ideal for basal application.
crop_type: All crops; especially Rice; Wheat; Pulses

uses: Supports balanced growth; enhances flowering and fruiting; strengthens roots
description: This NPK blend offers a high phosphorus and potassium ratio with moderate nitrogen, making it ideal for the reproductive stage of crops. It improves yield quality and stress tolerance.

uses: Enhances root and shoot development; supports early crop growth; increases tillering
description: A balanced NPK fertilizer with a higher phosphorus content suitable for basal application in most crops. It supports both vegetative and early reproductive growth stages.

uses: Provides equal proportions of N-P-K for all-round growth; suitable for foliar spray and fertigation
description: This fully water-soluble NPK fertilizer is widely used in drip and foliar applications. It ensures balanced nutrition throughout the crop cycle and is especially popular in horticulture.

uses: Promotes uniform growth; ideal for fertigation and foliar feeding; corrects multi-nutrient deficiency
description: A fully water-soluble grade suitable for all crops during active growth phases. Often used in fertigation systems and as a foliar spray to maintain balanced nutrition.

uses: Improves fruit quality and size; increases disease resistance; enhances water use efficiency
description: Muriate of Potash (0-0-60) is the most commonly used potassium fertilizer in India. It strengthens cell walls, improves drought tolerance, and enhances the shelf life of produce.

uses: Enhances fruit color and sweetness; safe for chloride-sensitive crops; improves crop quality
description: Sulphate of Potash (0-0-50) provides potassium along with sulphur and is recommended for chloride-sensitive crops like grapes, pomegranate, and potato. It improves yield and post-harvest quality.

uses: Supplies phosphorus and sulphur; improves root growth; corrects sulphur deficiency in soils
description: SSP (16% P2O5; 11% S) is a cost-effective source of both phosphorus and sulphur. It is widely used in India for oilseed and pulse crops where sulphur nutrition is equally important.

uses: Promotes vigorous root system; enhances phosphorus uptake; beneficial for phosphorus-deficient soils
description: TSP (46% P2O5) is a concentrated phosphatic fertilizer without nitrogen, ideal for use where only phosphorus supplementation is needed. It is suitable as a basal dose in most crops.

uses: Provides nitrogen and sulphur; ideal for alkaline soils; promotes leafy growth and greening
description: Ammonium Sulphate (21% N; 24% S) is a fast-acting nitrogen fertilizer with an acidifying effect, making it suitable for alkaline and calcareous soils. It is especially beneficial for sulphur-loving crops.

uses: Improves calcium nutrition; reduces soil acidity; provides quick-release nitrogen
description: CAN contains both ammoniacal and nitrate nitrogen (26% N) along with calcium, making it a safe and efficient nitrogen fertilizer. It is less prone to volatilization compared to urea and improves soil structure.

uses: Corrects zinc deficiency; improves grain filling and seed quality; enhances enzyme activity
description: Zinc Sulphate (21% Zn) is the most widely used micronutrient fertilizer in India, where zinc deficiency is widespread. It promotes healthy root growth, improves photosynthesis, and increases grain yield in cereals.

uses: Corrects iron chlorosis; improves chlorophyll synthesis; restores green color to yellowing plants
description: Ferrous Sulphate (19% Fe) is used to treat iron-deficiency chlorosis in crops and acidify high-pH soils. It is especially important in calcareous and waterlogged soils where iron availability is low.

uses: Improves pollen germination and fruit set; corrects boron deficiency; promotes cell division
description: Borax (11% B) is an essential micronutrient fertilizer that supports reproductive growth in crops. Boron deficiency leads to hollow heart in root crops, poor fruit set in orchards, and tip burn in vegetables.

uses: Corrects magnesium deficiency; enhances chlorophyll production; improves nutrient uptake
description: Magnesium Sulphate (9.6% Mg; 13% S) is used as both a soil and foliar application to correct magnesium deficiency, which commonly appears as interveinal chlorosis. It is especially useful in sandy and acidic soils.

uses: Improves soil structure and microbial activity; supplies balanced macro and micronutrients; enhances water retention
description: Vermicompost is produced by earthworm digestion of organic waste and is rich in humus, beneficial microbes, enzymes, and plant growth hormones. It improves soil fertility and promotes sustainable agriculture.
soil_type: All soil types; especially Sandy and degraded soils

uses: Improves soil organic matter; enhances microbial life; supplies slow-release nutrients
description: FYM is a mixture of animal dung, urine, and decomposed crop residues. When well-decomposed, it supplies a balanced range of nutrients and significantly improves soil water-holding capacity and aeration.

uses: Acts as natural pest deterrent; improves nitrogen efficiency; suppresses soil-borne pathogens
description: Neem Cake is the residue obtained after cold pressing of neem seeds. It contains azadirachtin which inhibits nematodes and soil insects, while also slowing down nitrification and improving fertilizer use efficiency.

uses: Promotes root and flower development; supplies phosphorus and calcium; slow-release nutrient source
description: Bone Meal is derived from ground animal bones and contains 15–27% phosphorus and 12–15% calcium. It is a slow-release fertilizer that improves root establishment and is especially popular in organic horticulture.

uses: Rapidly boosts soil nitrogen; corrects severe N-deficiency; promotes fast vegetative growth
description: Blood Meal (12–14% N) is a fast-acting organic nitrogen fertilizer derived from dried animal blood. It acidifies soil slightly and is useful for boosting green leafy growth in organic farming systems.

uses: Stimulates plant growth hormones; improves stress tolerance; enhances nutrient absorption
description: Seaweed extract contains cytokinins, auxins, and alginates that promote cell division and root development. It helps crops withstand drought, salinity, and frost stress while improving yield and quality.
crop_type: All crops; especially Horticulture; Grapes; Tomato

uses: Improves nutrient uptake efficiency; enhances soil carbon content; promotes root elongation
description: Humic Acid is derived from leonardite or lignite and improves cation exchange capacity (CEC) of soils. It chelates micronutrients and makes them more available to plants, particularly in high-pH soils.

uses: Provides potassium and nitrate nitrogen; improves fruit quality; suitable for fertigation and foliar spray
description: Potassium Nitrate is a fully water-soluble fertilizer widely used in drip irrigation and foliar applications. The nitrate form of nitrogen is rapidly absorbed, and the high potassium content improves fruit size, color, and sugar content.

uses: Supplies highly soluble phosphorus and nitrogen; ideal as starter fertilizer; enhances root and seedling development
description: MAP is a fully water-soluble fertilizer with the highest phosphorus content among common water-soluble fertilizers. Its low pH in solution makes phosphorus highly available, especially in alkaline soils.

uses: Boosts root strength and fruit quality; provides phosphorus and potassium without nitrogen; ideal during flowering
description: MKP is a premium water-soluble fertilizer used in fertigation and foliar spray during flowering and fruiting. It is chloride-free and supports high-value crops where chloride toxicity is a concern.

uses: Supplies calcium for strong cell walls; prevents blossom-end rot; provides fast-acting nitrogen
description: Calcium Nitrate is a fully water-soluble fertilizer used primarily in fertigation. It supplies both calcium and nitrate nitrogen, preventing disorders like blossom-end rot in tomato and tip burn in lettuce.

uses: Provides dual-form nitrogen for sustained release; promotes vigorous vegetative growth
description: Ammonium Nitrate (34% N) contains both nitrate and ammoniacal nitrogen, offering both quick and slow nitrogen release. It is effective across a wide range of soil types but must be handled with care due to its hygroscopic nature.

uses: Corrects sulphur deficiency; acidifies alkaline soils; improves protein and oil content in crops
description: Elemental Sulphur (90% S) is used to lower soil pH in alkaline and saline-sodic soils and to supply sulphur nutrition to crops like oilseeds and pulses. It is oxidized in soil by bacteria to form sulphate, which is then available to plants.

uses: Corrects copper deficiency; suppresses fungal diseases in soil; improves enzyme activity
description: Copper Sulphate (25% Cu) is used both as a micronutrient fertilizer and a fungicide. Copper deficiency is common in peat soils and causes dieback and wilting; copper application restores normal growth and photosynthesis.

uses: Corrects manganese deficiency; improves photosynthesis and nitrogen assimilation
description: Manganese Sulphate (30–32% Mn) is used to treat manganese deficiency that appears as interveinal chlorosis in young leaves. It is commonly applied as a foliar spray for quick correction in neutral to alkaline soils.

uses: Promotes nitrogen fixation in legumes; corrects molybdenum deficiency; improves protein synthesis
description: Sodium Molybdate is used in very small quantities to correct molybdenum deficiency, which is critical for nitrogen fixation in legumes and nitrate reductase activity. Seed treatment with molybdenum is a common and cost-effective practice.

uses: Fixes atmospheric nitrogen symbiotically in legume root nodules; reduces chemical nitrogen requirement
description: Rhizobium is a nitrogen-fixing bacterial biofertilizer that forms symbiotic nodules on legume roots. It can fix 50–200 kg N/ha per season, significantly reducing the need for chemical nitrogen fertilizers.

uses: Fixes free-living atmospheric nitrogen; promotes root growth through auxin production
description: Azospirillum is a free-living nitrogen-fixing bacteria used for non-leguminous crops. It also produces growth-promoting hormones that enhance root branching and nutrient uptake efficiency.

uses: Solubilizes fixed soil phosphorus; improves phosphorus availability; reduces phosphatic fertilizer usage
description: PSB inoculants contain bacteria like Bacillus and Pseudomonas that convert insoluble soil phosphorus compounds into plant-available forms. Regular use reduces chemical phosphorus application by 20–25%.
soil_type: All soil types; especially P-fixing soils

uses: Extends root absorption zone; improves phosphorus and water uptake; enhances stress tolerance
description: Vesicular Arbuscular Mycorrhiza (VAM) forms symbiotic associations with plant roots and dramatically increases the root absorption surface area. It is especially effective in phosphorus-deficient and drought-prone soils.

uses: Adds nitrogen to soil through incorporation; improves organic matter; suppresses weeds
description: Dhaincha (Sesbania bispinosa) is one of the most popular green manure crops in India. It fixes 80–100 kg N/ha and when incorporated before flowering significantly enriches soil organic matter and nitrogen status.

uses: Improves soil organic matter; supplies nitrogen; deters nematodes and soil pests
description: Castor Cake is the residue after oil extraction from castor seeds. It contains about 4–5% N and acts as a slow-release organic nitrogen fertilizer. It also has pesticidal properties that suppress soil nematodes.

uses: Supplies nitrogen; improves soil microbial activity; enhances soil moisture retention
description: Groundnut Cake (6–8% N; 1.5% P) is a rich organic fertilizer obtained after oil extraction. It decomposes relatively quickly in soil and is a popular organic nutrient source in South India.

uses: Provides high nitrogen and phosphorus; improves soil biology; acts as fast-release organic fertilizer
description: Dried poultry manure contains 3–4% N; 2.5–3% P2O5 and 2% K2O, making it one of the richest organic manures available. It is particularly effective for vegetable crops and improves soil microbial populations significantly.

uses: Provides fast-acting organic nitrogen; supplies amino acids and micronutrients; promotes microbial activity
description: Fish Meal contains 8–12% N along with phosphorus, calcium, and trace minerals. It decomposes quickly and is a valuable input in organic vegetable and fruit farming, especially in coastal regions.

uses: Improves soil structure and moisture retention; recycles organic waste; provides balanced slow-release nutrients
description: Quality compost contains 1–2% N; 0.5–1% P and 0.5–1.5% K along with humus and beneficial microorganisms. Long-term use rebuilds degraded soil fertility and is fundamental to sustainable farming.
crop_type: All crops; especially Vegetables and Orchards

uses: Improves soil CEC and water retention; enhances potassium and micronutrient availability; stimulates root growth
description: Potassium Humate is derived from weathered leonardite and provides both humic acids and soluble potassium. It chelates soil nutrients, improves soil aggregation, and is particularly effective in saline and compacted soils.

uses: Stimulates plant metabolism; improves stress recovery; enhances nutrient absorption and protein synthesis
description: Hydrolyzed amino acid fertilizers provide free L-amino acids that are directly absorbed by plants through leaves and roots. They improve nitrogen use efficiency, enhance enzyme activity, and help crops recover from abiotic stress.
crop_type: All crops; especially Horticulture; Flowers; Fruits

uses: Strengthens cell walls and improves lodging resistance; enhances pest and disease resistance
description: Calcium Silicate supplies silicon and calcium to crops, strengthening epidermal cell walls which makes plants more resistant to fungal infections, insect damage, and lodging. It is highly recommended for paddy cultivation.

uses: Reclaims sodic soils; supplies calcium and sulphur; improves soil structure in clay soils
description: Gypsum (23% Ca; 18% S) is a valuable soil amendment that replaces exchangeable sodium in sodic soils, improving soil structure and drainage. It also supplies sulphur and calcium without affecting soil pH.
crop_type: All crops; especially Groundnut; Oilseeds; Pulses

uses: Corrects soil acidity; improves phosphorus and molybdenum availability; promotes beneficial soil microorganisms
description: Agricultural Lime (CaCO3) is the primary material used to raise soil pH in acidic soils. It neutralizes soil acidity, improves the availability of essential nutrients, and creates favorable conditions for nitrogen-fixing bacteria.
crop_type: All crops grown on acidic soils

uses: Corrects soil acidity while supplying both calcium and magnesium; improves soil structure
description: Dolomite contains approximately 21% Ca and 11% Mg and is preferred over calcitic lime when soils are also deficient in magnesium. It is a slow-acting liming material that provides sustained pH correction.

uses: Provides balanced equal-ratio N-P-K nutrition; suitable for maintenance fertilization; versatile for multiple crops
description: NPK 15-15-15 is a general-purpose compound fertilizer offering equal and balanced nutrition. It is commonly used as a basal dose in diverse crops where soil nutrient status is moderate and balanced nutrition is needed.

uses: Corrects multiple micronutrient deficiencies simultaneously; highly bioavailable; effective in high-pH soils
description: EDTA-chelated micronutrient mixes supply Zn; Fe; Mn; Cu; and B in a form that resists fixation in alkaline soils. They are used as foliar sprays or soil applications for rapid correction of complex deficiencies.
